title: Damis
wikitext:
{{External|rs}} {{Otheruses|the quest boss fought during [[Desert Treasure I]]|the [[Nightmare Zone]] variant|Damis (Nightmare Zone)}} {{Infobox Monster |version1 = First form |version2 = Second form |dropversion2 = Second form |name1 = Damis |name2 = Damis |image1 = [[File:Damis first form.png|150px]] |image2 = [[File:Damis.png|150px]] |release1 = [[18 April]] [[2005]] |release2 = [[18 April]] [[2005]] |update1 = Desert Treasure |update2 = Desert Treasure |members = Yes |combat1 = 103 |combat2 = 174 |size = 1 |examine = The warrior of darkness. |xpbonus1 = 5 |xpbonus2 = 5 |max hit1 = 22 |max hit2 = 28 |aggressive = Yes |poisonous = No |attack style = [[Crush]] |attack speed1 = 4 |attack speed2 = 3 |hitpoints1 = 90 |hitpoints2 = 200 |att1 = 90 |att2 = 160 |str1 = 90 |str2 = 100 |str3 = 198 |str4 = 200 |def1 = 90 |def2 = 160 |def3 = 90 |def4 = 160 |mage = 1 |range = 1 |attbns = 0 |strbns1 = 80 |strbns2 = 100 |amagic = 0 |mbns = 0 |arange = 0 |rngbns = 0 |dstab1 = 60 |dstab2 = 100 |dslash1 = 60 |dslash2 = 100 |dcrush1 = 60 |dcrush2 = 100 |dmagic1 = 60 |dmagic2 = 80 |drange1 = 60 |drange2 = 120 |immunepoison = No |immunevenom = No |id1 = 682 |id2 = 683 }} {{Synced switch |version1 = [[File:Damis first form chathead.png|left]] |version2 = [[File:Damis second form chathead.png|left]] |version3 = [[File:Damis first form chathead.png|left]] |version4 = [[File:Damis second form chathead.png|left]] }} '''Damis''' is one of the four guardians of the [[Diamonds of Azzanadra]], fought only when doing the [[Desert Treasure I]] quest. He is the only guardian who exclusively uses [[melee]] to attack, however this is made up by him having two forms, with the second form being able to attack at a quick rate as well as draining [[Prayer]] rapidly. As with most monsters in the [[Shadow Dungeon]], Damis allows other monsters to pass through him. He can be fought in the [[Nightmare Zone]] after the quest is complete. ==Location== [[File:Shadow Dungeon map.png|240px|left|thumb|The Shadow Dungeon.]] He is found at the end of the Shadow Dungeon, which is accessed via a ladder requiring the [[ring of visibility]] to see. When approaching Damis' location, the player will be told in their chatbox that they hear a voice from the walls telling them to turn back. (Note: If you die, you will be unable to retrieve your items from your gravestone without another [[ring of visibility]] you will have to retrieve them from Death's Office). {{clear|left}} ==Strategy== [[File:Desert treasure safespot.png|thumb|left|A player using a [[giant rat]] to [[safespot]] Damis.]] Much harder than mid-level quest bosses like Arrg, Damis has higher Defence against melee and Ranged, so it is recommended to use Magic. [[Iban's staff]], [[trident of the seas]], or [[trident of the swamp]] are all effective options. [[Protect from Melee]] will mitigate all damage from Damis, however, his second form has higher stats, attacks faster, and drains Prayer rapidly when within melee distance. The best method to defeat Damis is by [[safespot]]ting him, get the aggression of a [[bat]] or [[giant rat]] found nearby and lure Damis behind alongside the other aggressive monsters. Only the bat and giant rats will prevent Damis and the other aggressive monsters from pathing to the player. An alternative method to using the bat or giant rat is to use the assistance of another player to block Damis' path. Using a binding spell such as [[Snare]] or [[Entangle]] on Damis or other monsters in the area may also be helpful. If the player is struggling to safe spot Damis, they can bring a lot of prayer potions and use protect from melee to fight him directly. The prayer drain effect makes it difficult to melee Damis in a multi-combat zone because of damage from other aggressive monsters. By engaging Damis alone, it may be possible to rely on high-healing food to reclaim hit points instead of prayer potions to protect from damage. {{clear|left}} ==Quotes== During Damis' second form, he may say one of the following quotes per [[RuneScape clock|game tick]] when he is close up to the player: *{{overhead|I will crush you utterly!}} *{{overhead|You are no match for my power!}} *{{overhead|Taste my blade and feel my wrath!}} *{{overhead|I... am... invincible!}} *{{overhead|Your pathetic skill won't help you now!}} (when having [[Protect from Melee]] active) *{{overhead|You will regret ever coming here!}} *{{overhead|Hah! I fear not your feeble power!}} ==Drops== ===100%=== {{DropsTableHead|leagueRegion=No}} {{DropsLine|name=Shadow diamond|quantity=1|rarity=Always|raritynotes=Only dropped when fought during [[Desert Treasure I]].|gemw=No|dropversion=Second form}} {{DropsTableBottom}} {{Reflist|group=d}} ==Changes== {{Subject changes header}} {{Subject changes |date = 31 May 2005 |update = New Emotes And Other Tweaks |change = The NPC's sword was graphically updated. }} {{Subject changes footer}} ===Gallery (historical)=== {{Switch infobox |text1 = First form |item1 = |text2 = Second form |item2 = }} {|style="text-align: center" cellpadding="15" | {{Synced switch |version1 = [[File:Damis first form (historical).png|center|300x150px]] |version2 = [[File:Damis (historical).png|center|300x150px]] }} | {{Synced switch |version1 = [[File:Damis first form.png|center|300x150px]] |version2 = [[File:Damis.png|center|300x150px]] }} |- |18 April 2005 –
31 May 2005 |8 May 2007 –
present |} ==Trivia== *Damis (دامِسٌ) is an Arabic word meaning "dark" or "gloomy", used to describe a severe absence of light. *Damis is the only boss from [[Desert Treasure I]] that does not appear in the [[Recipe for Disaster]] quest, likely because his prayer draining ability would be rendered useless due to the boss fight not allowing prayer. *Players believed Damis was specifically weak to earth spells, but this was proven incorrect by Mod Ash.{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1028980496020242432|date=13 August 2018|archiveurl=https://archive.is/9U1cP|archivedate=28 May 2020|quote=The ice & fire warriors have effects. So do mummies in the pyramid. That's all I could find.}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Desert Treasure I}} {{Shadow Dungeon}} [[Category:Bosses]] [[Category:Quest monsters]] [[Category:Monsters with multiple forms]] [[Category:Zamorakians]]
